answer:
\boxed{B}

The **Micropaedia** of the **15th edition** (1974–1980) of the *Encyclopedia Britannica* was published in **10 volumes**, serving as the first part of the three-part set (followed by the **Macropaedia** and **Propaedia**). This structure was later revised in later editions, but the 15th edition remains the most commonly referenced version for this detail.
